job_statement: When I need to deploy a Spring Boot application to OpenShift, I want to use Argo CD to automate the deployment from a Git repository, so that I can have a reliable, version-controlled deployment process.
pain_points: Manual deployment processes are error-prone and time-consuming
Need to ensure consistency between Git repository and cluster state
Require visibility into deployment status and history
context: Developer has a Spring Boot application code stored in Git and needs to deploy it to an OpenShift cluster using GitOps practices with Argo CD
success_criteria: Application is successfully deployed to OpenShift cluster
Deployment is automated and repeatable
Application state matches Git repository definition
evidence: deploying-a-spring-boot-application-with-argo-cd.adoc -> Assembly 'Deploying a Spring Boot application with Argo CD', lines 1-21 [assembly context and prerequisites]
notes: Source assembly: cicd/gitops/deploying-a-spring-boot-application-with-argo-cd.adoc. This is a high-level job that encompasses the entire workflow of deploying applications with Argo CD.

job_statement: When I need to create and configure an Argo CD application, I want to use the Argo CD dashboard to define the application parameters visually, so that I can easily configure the Git repository, sync policy, and deployment destination without writing YAML.
pain_points: Need to remember correct YAML syntax for application definitions
Want visual feedback while configuring applications
May not be familiar with all available configuration options
context: Developer is logged into Argo CD and wants to create a new application deployment using the web-based dashboard interface
success_criteria: Application is created with correct Git repository URL and path
Sync policy is properly configured (manual or automatic)
Application is associated with the correct namespace and cluster destination
Application appears in Argo CD dashboard after creation
evidence: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc -> Section 'Creating an application by using the Argo CD dashboard', lines 13-59 [module: gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc (PROCEDURE). Provides GUI-based approach to application creation. Configuration includes Application Name, Project, Sync Policy, Repository URL, Revision, Path, Destination, and Namespace.

job_statement: When I need to create an Argo CD application from the command line, I want to use the oc tool with application YAML definitions, so that I can automate application creation and integrate it into scripts or CI/CD pipelines.
pain_points: GUI-based configuration doesn't fit into automated workflows
Need to version control application configurations
Want to create multiple applications programmatically
context: Engineer wants to create Argo CD applications using command-line tools and YAML manifests, enabling automation and version control
success_criteria: Application is created from YAML definition file
Application appears in Argo CD and can be managed
Namespace is properly labeled for Argo CD management
Process can be scripted and automated
evidence: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-oc-tool.adoc -> Section 'Creating an application by using the oc tool', lines 13-68 [module: gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-oc-tool.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-oc-tool.adoc (PROCEDURE). Demonstrates declarative approach using oc create command with YAML files. Includes steps to clone sample repository, create application from YAML, verify creation, and label namespace.

job_statement: When I need to ensure my namespace can be managed by Argo CD, I want to apply the correct label to the namespace, so that the Argo CD instance has permission to deploy and manage resources in that namespace.
pain_points: Argo CD deployments fail without proper namespace labels
Need to understand RBAC and namespace management
Manual namespace configuration is error-prone
context: After creating an Argo CD application, the target namespace must be labeled to grant Argo CD permission to manage resources within it
success_criteria: Namespace has the correct label: argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by=openshift-gitops
Argo CD can successfully deploy resources to the namespace
Application sync operations succeed
notes: Critical configuration step mentioned in both dashboard and oc tool procedures. Label format: 'argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by=openshift-gitops'. Can be done via web console or oc label command.

job_statement: When I want to understand how Argo CD maintains application consistency, I want to test the self-healing behavior by making manual changes to deployed resources, so that I can verify Argo CD will automatically correct drift from the Git repository.
pain_points: Uncertainty about whether Argo CD will detect and correct manual changes
Need to understand self-healing mechanism before relying on it
Want to verify automatic sync is working correctly
context: Developer has deployed an application with Argo CD and wants to test that the self-healing feature works by intentionally creating drift between cluster state and Git repository
success_criteria: Manual changes to deployment are detected by Argo CD
Argo CD automatically reverts changes to match Git repository
Events in Argo CD dashboard show detection and correction of drift
Application maintains 'Synced' status after auto-healing
evidence: modules/gitops-verifying-argo-cd-self-healing-behavior.adoc -> Section 'Verifying Argo CD self-healing behavior', lines 5-49 [module: gitops-verifying-argo-cd-self-healing-behavior.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-verifying-argo-cd-self-healing-behavior.adoc (PROCEDURE). Demonstrates self-healing by scaling deployment from 1 to 2 pods and observing automatic scale-down back to 1 pod. Events tab in Argo CD shows detection and correction.

job_statement: When I need to modify my deployed application, I want to commit changes to the Git repository, so that Argo CD will automatically detect and deploy the updates to my cluster.
pain_points: Need to ensure changes are deployed without manual intervention
Want to maintain GitOps principles for all changes
Require visibility into when changes are detected and applied
context: Developer needs to update application configuration or code and wants to leverage GitOps workflow where Git is the single source of truth
success_criteria: Changes committed to Git repository
Argo CD detects changes in configured path
Application is automatically synced with new changes
Updated resources are deployed to cluster
notes: Core GitOps workflow: modify files in Git repository (like deployment.yaml), commit changes, Argo CD detects and deploys automatically. Example shows changing failureThreshold value in deployment configuration.

job_statement: When I need to verify my Argo CD application is properly synchronized, I want to view the application status in the Argo CD dashboard, so that I can confirm the deployed state matches the Git repository and identify any sync issues.
pain_points: Need quick visibility into application sync status
Want to identify what resources are deployed
Require insight into any differences between Git and cluster state
context: Developer or operator wants to check the current state of their Argo CD application and ensure it is properly synchronized
success_criteria: Application shows 'Synced' status in dashboard
All deployed resources are visible in application view
No out-of-sync resources are detected
Application health status is shown
notes: Dashboard provides visual representation of application state, resources, and sync status. Users can drill down into individual resources to see details.

job_statement: When I need to troubleshoot Argo CD sync issues or understand application lifecycle events, I want to view the application events in the Argo CD dashboard, so that I can see when Argo CD detected changes, performed syncs, or encountered errors.
pain_points: Difficult to diagnose why syncs are failing without event history
Need timeline of Argo CD actions for debugging
Want to understand when and why Argo CD made changes
context: Engineer is investigating application behavior, sync failures, or self-healing actions and needs detailed event history
success_criteria: Event timeline is visible in Argo CD dashboard
Events show detection of out-of-sync resources
Events show sync/resync actions taken by Argo CD
Timestamps help correlate events with application changes
evidence: modules/gitops-verifying-argo-cd-self-healing-behavior.adoc -> lines 48-49 [accessing EVENTS tab in APP DETAILS]
notes: Events tab accessible via application tile -> APP DETAILS -> EVENTS. Shows detection of drift and resyncing actions. Useful for troubleshooting and understanding Argo CD behavior.
